idea_summary: Adapted iter105 from 16416x16416x16416, cp.async + WMMA 2-stage pipeline
Smaller 64x64 tiles + 4-stage pipeline - reduced compute intensity, slower
128x128 tiles with --maxrregcount=128 for slightly better occupancy
128x256 tiles with BK=16, larger output tile but too many K iterations
Added #pragma unroll 4 to K loop, reduced bank conflicts
BK=64 exceeds shared memory limit (68KB vs 48KB max)
Warp specialization verification failed - mapping incorrect
CTA swizzle 4x4 pattern - slightly worse than baseline
BK=48 exceeds shared memory limit (51KB vs 48KB)
Split-K with atomics - verification failed and slow
Register double-buffering increased pressure, slower than baseline
Single-stage loses pipelining benefit, much slower
Loop unroll 8 increased register pressure, slightly slower
